args.query: site:xfinity.com support prepare for technician appointment Xfinity what to do before technician visit

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Prepare for a service visit

  - url: https://www.xfinity.com/support/articles/prepare-for-your-service-visit

  - source: Xfinity

  - domain: xfinity.com


  ## Content


  # Prepare for a service visit


  Xfinity recommends reviewing a few practical details before a technician visit.


  ## Appointment timing


  Service appointments are scheduled in two-hour windows. The technician may arrive
  at any point between the beginning and the end of the assigned window, so the full
  timeframe should be kept available.


  Customers who receive appointment text updates may get status information about
  the visit and the technician''s arrival time. Xfinity notes that the technician
  does not normally call before arriving.


  ## Have an adult available


  For a standard in-home service visit, an adult should be present. The technician
  may ask for photo identification. If only a minor is present, the technician cannot
  enter the home. If the adult who is responsible for the visit leaves while work
  is in progress, the technician may have to stop the visit and leave.


  ## Make the work area accessible


  Before the visit, make sure the technician can reach the equipment and any areas
  that may need to be inspected. Clear access can help the technician work on the
  service problem without unnecessary delays.


  ## Appointment access


  Xfinity lists multiple channels for accessing appointment information, including
  text messages, the customer''s online account, supported Xfinity TV interfaces,
  and Xfinity Assistant. These channels can be used to review appointment-related
  information and reach the provider''s appointment-management experience.


  The page also points customers to the separate Xfinity appointment-management guide
  for detailed self-service appointment controls. It also references the Appointment
  Waitlist, which may be available for customers interested in earlier time options.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Prepare for a service visit
url: https://www.xfinity.com/support/articles/prepare-for-your-service-visit

Your Xfinity technician visit is **confirmed**:

- **Service:** Internet  
- **Date:** Next Wednesday  
- **Arrival window:** **2:00–4:00 PM local time**

What Xfinity expects you to do next:

1. **Keep the entire 2–4 PM window available**—the technician may arrive at any point during it.
2. **Have an adult present for the full visit.** The technician may request photo ID, and the visit can’t proceed if only a minor is home.
3. **Make the equipment and relevant areas accessible**—for example, clear access to the modem, wiring, outlets, and any areas the technician may need to inspect.
4. **Watch for Xfinity text updates** if you’re enrolled in appointment notifications. Xfinity says technicians don’t normally call before arriving.

You don’t appear to need to take any additional scheduling action unless you need to change the appointment. [Xfinity’s preparation guidance](https://www.xfinity.com/support/articles/prepare-for-your-service-visit)

final_answer:
Your Xfinity technician visit is **confirmed**:

- **Service:** Internet  
- **Date:** Next Wednesday  
- **Arrival window:** **2:00–4:00 PM local time**

What Xfinity expects you to do next:

1. **Keep the entire 2–4 PM window available**—the technician may arrive at any point during it.
2. **Have an adult present for the full visit.** The technician may request photo ID, and the visit can’t proceed if only a minor is home.
3. **Make the equipment and relevant areas accessible**—for example, clear access to the modem, wiring, outlets, and any areas the technician may need to inspect.
4. **Watch for Xfinity text updates** if you’re enrolled in appointment notifications. Xfinity says technicians don’t normally call before arriving.

You don’t appear to need to take any additional scheduling action unless you need to change the appointment. [Xfinity’s preparation guidance](https://www.xfinity.com/support/articles/prepare-for-your-service-visit)
